summary: This is a brief pointer post directing readers to Joel's full article about phone screening candidates. The core premise is that phone screens serve as an efficient filter to avoid wasting time and money on in-person interviews with candidates who clearly aren't qualified. The post itself contains only a single quote and a link to the detailed article.
key_points: Phone screens should be used as a pre-filter before investing in full in-person interviews
The primary goal is efficiency: avoiding wasted time and money on obviously unqualified candidates
Phone screens are a standard part of a structured hiring pipeline
money_quotes: Before moving on to a full-fledged in-person interview, we usually use a phone screen to make sure that we're not wasting time and money on someone who is just seriously not smart.
key_insight: Phone screens are an essential efficiency tool in hiring, filtering out clearly unqualified candidates before committing to costly in-person interviews.
